nameMeanings.name: Kent
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names В© Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: habitational name from the county of Kent. The surname is also established in Ireland, where English bearers of this name settled in Meath, in the 13th century. The name has been Gaelicized as Ceannt.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names В© Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrЕҚd вҖҳfameвҖҷ + berht вҖҳbright, famousвҖҷ, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274вҖ“1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

en.lifeSummary: When Robert Kent was born about 1594, in Winterbourne Dauntsey, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Thomas Kent, was 47 and his mother, Elizabeth Wilson, was 42. He had at least 4 sons and 3 daughters with Dorothy Prewett.

ru.lifeSummary: РҡРҫРіРҙР° Robert Kent СҖРҫРҙРёР»СҒСҸ РҫРәРҫР»Рҫ 1594 Рі. РІ РјРөСҒСӮРҪРҫСҒСӮРё РІРёРҪСӮРёСҖРұСғСҖРҪ РҙР°СғРҪСҶРё, СғРёР»СҶС…РёСҖ, РҗРҪРіР»РёСҸ, Р’РөР»РёРәРҫРұСҖРёСӮР°РҪРёСҸ, РөРіРҫ РҫСӮСҶСғ РҝРҫ РёРјРөРҪРё Thomas Kent РұСӢР»Рҫ 47 Р»РөСӮ, Р° РјР°СӮРөСҖРё, РәРҫСӮРҫСҖСғСҺ Р·РІР°Р»Рё Elizabeth Wilson, 42. РЈ РҪРөРіРҫ РұСӢР»Рҫ РҝРҫ РәСҖР°Р№РҪРөР№ РјРөСҖРө 4 СҒСӢРҪР° Рё 3 РҙРҫСҮРөСҖРё РҫСӮ Dorothy Prewett.
